species: ANME-2b archaea consortium (CO2-reduction to methane pathway, no sulfate)

growth_records.strain: environmental enrichment from push core PC6, Jaco Scar seep, Costa Rica margin
growth_records.medium: Artificial seawater (ASW) medium overlaying sediment slurry, CO/bicarbonate condition (no sulfate)

sec.compound: methane (CH4), produced via CO2 reduction using CO as electron donor
sec.method: reported (Table 2, bulk sediment rate); approx. 1/9 of the parallel CH4/sulfate condition rate (0.52 ± 0.02 nmol CH4 d-1 cm-3 sed)

growth_records.cite: Guo et al. Carbon monoxide oxidation expands the known metabolic capacity in anaerobic methanotrophic consortia. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-71433-9. [PMC13079720]
growth_records.snippet: CH4 production or oxidation rate (nmol CH4 d−1 cm−3sed): CO/bicarbonate 0.061 ± 0.054; CH4/sulfate 0.52 ± 0.02 (Table 2).
growth_records.notes: From Table 2, at 0.4 bar CO. Methane production (methanogenesis from CO2 reduction, using CO as electron donor) by ANME-2b in absence of methane/sulfate. Not biomass-normalized; mixed uncultured consortium.
